INTERVENORS HAVE MET THEIR BURDEN. THEY HAVE SHOWN THAT THEY HAVE A DIRECT INTEREST IN THE ISSUES PRESENTED IN THIS CASE, PARTICULARLY AS TO QUESTIONS OF STATUTORY AND REGULATORY INTERPRETATION WHICH HAVE A DIRECT EFFECT ON CERTIFICATION TESTING PROCEDURES THAT INTERVENORS WILL BE SUBJECT TO IN THE FUTURE. MOREOVER, CARB, AS THE REGULATING AGENCY AND PLAINTIFF, AS A COMPETITOR OF INTERVENORS ARE NOT APPROPRIATE PARTIES TO REPRESENT INTERVENORS' INTERESTS IN THIS CASE. TIMELY APPLICATION HAS BEEN MADE AND INTERVENTION WILL NOT ENLARGE THE ISSUES IN THIS CASE. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER.) JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF NAVISTAR, INC.'S MOTION TO RECONSIDER RULING ON ENGINE MANUFACTURERS ASSOCIATION'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE COMPLAINT IN INTERVENTION. COUNSEL APPEARED WHEN THE ACCOMPANYING DISCOVERY MATTERS WERE INITIALLY CALLED ON THE 9:00 AM DISCOVERY CALENDAR, AND INDICATED THEY ARE SUBMITTING TO THE COURT'S TENTATIVE RULING AS TO THIS MOTION. BASED ON THIS REPRESENTATION, WHEN THE COURT CALLED THIS MATTER ON THE LAW & MOTION CALENDAR, JUDGE WOOLARD ADOPTED THE TENTATIVE RULING ON BEHALF OF JUDGE LORETTA GIORGI. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION OF THE COURT'S ORDER GRANTING EMA LEAVE TO INTERVENE IS DENIED. FIRST, PLAINTIFF'S RELIANCE ON THE DEPOSITION TESTIMONY OF EMA PRESIDENT MANDEL IS MISPLACED. PLAINTIFF ALLEGES THAT MR. MANDEL ADMITTED THAT EMA HAS NO DIRECT OR IMMEDIATE INTEREST IN THIS CASE. HOWEVER, THIS ALLEGATION IS TAKEN OUT OF CONTEXT. MR. MANDEL IN FACT DID TESTIFY TO THE IMPLICATION OF THIS CASE'S ADJUDICATION ON OTHER NON-ROAD ENGINE MANUFACTURERS. THEREFORE, THERE IS NO NEW MATTER PRESENTED TO WARRANT REVERSAL OF THE COURT'S ORIGINAL ORDER. PLAINTIFF'S ADDITIONAL ARGUMENT THAT EMA VIOLATED ITS INTERNAL RULES BY NOT POLLING EVERY MEMBER OF THE EMA BEFORE IT PARTICIPATED IN THIS LAWSUIT IS ALSO WITHOUT MERIT. MR. MANDEL'S DEPOSITION TRANSCRIPT INDICATES THAT 27 OF THE 28 MEMBERS WERE BALLOTED. THE ONLY MEMBER IT DID NOT BALLOT WAS AN EMPLOYEE OF PLAINTIFF SERVING AS A BOARD MEMBER. ACCORDING TO EMA RULES IT WAS PROPER TO EXCLUDE THIS MEMBER WHO HAD A CONFLICT OF INTEREST IN THIS VOTE. THE COURT FINDS THAT EMA DID NOT AGREE TO WITHDRAWAL FROM THE LITIGATION IN THAT THE PARTIES DID NOT REACH A STIPULATION. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Reconsider Ruling Motion For Leave To File Complaint In Internvention; Notice Of Lodgemnt
Off Calendar Sep-22-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF NAVISTAR, INC.'S MOTION TO RECONSIDER RULING ON ENGINE MANUFACTURERS ASSOCIATION'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE COMPLAINT IN INTERVENTION.
Counsel appeared when the accompanying discovery matters were initially called on the 9:00 am Discovery calendar, and indicated they are submitting to the court's tentative ruling as to this motion. Based on this representation, when the court called this matter on the Law & Motion calendar, Judge Woolard adopted the tentative ruling on behalf of Judge Loretta Giorgi. The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff's Motion for reconsideration of the Court's Order granting EMA leave to intervene is denied. First, Plaintiff's reliance on the deposition testimony of EMA President Mandel is misplaced. Plaintiff alleges that Mr. Mandel admitted that EMA has no direct or immediate interest in this case. However, this allegation is taken out of context. Mr. Mandel in fact did testify to the implication of this case's adjudication on other non-road engine manufacturers. Therefore, there is no new matter presented to warrant reversal of the Court's original order.
Plaintiff's additional argument that EMA violated its internal rules by not polling every member of the EMA before it participated in this lawsuit is also without merit. Mr. Mandel's deposition transcript indicates that 27 of the 28 members were balloted. The only member it did not ballot was an employee of Plaintiff serving as a board member. According to EMA rules it was proper to exclude this member who had a conflict of interest in this vote.
The Court finds that EMA did not agree to withdrawal from the litigation in that the parties did not reach a stipulation. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. (302/LMG) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998

Off Calendar May-05-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
INTERVENORS CUMMINS INC., DAIMLER TRUCKS NORTH AMERICA LLC, DETROIT DIESEL CORPORATION, MACK TRUCKS, INC. AND VOLVO GROUP NORTH AMERICA, LLC'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE COMPLAINT IN INTERVENTION.

RULING: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling - Cummin, Inc., Daimler Trucks North America, LLC, Detroit Diesel Corp., Mack Trucks, Inc., and Volvo Group North America, LLC's Motion to Intervene is granted Per CCP 387(a) and (b). Intervenors have met their burden. They have shown that they have a direct interest in the issues presented in this case, particularly as to questions of statutory and regulatory interpretation which have a direct effect on certification testing procedures that Intervenors will be subject to in the future. Moreover, CARB, as the Regulating Agency and Plaintiff, as a competitor of Intervenors are not appropriate parties to represent Intervenors' interests in this case. Timely application has been made and intervention will not enlarge the issues in this case.
(PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER.) (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998
